description: Full MCQ question bank snapshot (no answers recorded)

questions.question: Which algorithm is used in Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (BKT) to model student learning?
questions.explanation: BKT uses a Hidden Markov Model (HMM) to represent the latent knowledge state of a student as a binary variable (known/unknown) that evolves over time.

questions.question: In K-Means clustering, what does the algorithm minimise?
questions.explanation: K-Means iteratively assigns points to their nearest centroid and updates centroids to minimise the within-cluster sum of squared Euclidean distances (inertia).

questions.question: Deep Knowledge Tracing (DKT) uses which type of neural network to model knowledge state?
questions.explanation: DKT employs a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network to capture temporal dependencies in student response sequences and predict future performance.

questions.question: Which MediaPipe solution is primarily used for real-time hand-landmark detection?
questions.explanation: MediaPipe Hands provides a pipeline that detects and tracks 21 3D hand landmarks per hand in real time, enabling gesture-based interaction.

questions.question: What does the OpenCV function cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY) do?

options.A: Converts a grayscale image to BGR
options.B: Applies Gaussian blur to the image
options.C: Converts a BGR colour image to grayscale
options.D: Resizes the image to a fixed dimension

questions.explanation: cv2.cvtColor with the COLOR_BGR2GRAY flag converts a 3-channel BGR image to a single-channel grayscale image using the standard luminosity formula.

questions.question: PR-AUC (Area Under the Precision-Recall Curve) is especially useful when:

options.C: The positive class is rare (class imbalance)
options.D: The model outputs discrete labels only

questions.explanation: PR-AUC is preferred over ROC-AUC for imbalanced datasets because it focuses on the performance of the minority (positive) class without being skewed by the large number of true negatives.

questions.question: In digital communications, what does the acronym ECE stand for?
questions.explanation: ECE stands for Electronics and Communication Engineering, a discipline combining electronic circuit design with communication systems and signal processing.

questions.question: In an AR tutoring system, which rendering pipeline component overlays virtual content on the real world?
questions.explanation: Pose estimation and registration tracks the camera's position and orientation relative to real-world markers, enabling accurate overlay of virtual objects onto the live camera feed.

questions.question: Which metric is defined as 2 × (Precision × Recall) / (Precision + Recall)?
questions.explanation: The F1 Score is the harmonic mean of Precision and Recall, providing a single balanced measure that penalises extreme values in either metric.

questions.question: What is the primary role of a convolutional layer in a CNN?

options.A: Reduce overfitting by randomly dropping neurons
options.B: Learn spatial feature maps via learnable filters
options.D: Flatten the input tensor into a 1-D vector

questions.explanation: A convolutional layer applies a set of learnable filters (kernels) across the input, producing feature maps that capture local spatial patterns such as edges, textures, and shapes.
